mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents with a focus on standardized communication protocols. It simplifies deployment complexity, enhances security, and reduces operational overhead. Ideal for organizations looking to implement AI agents in a production environment with a robust infrastructure.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is necessary for managing the Kagenti deployment.

```bash

curl -Lo kagenti-cli https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.4.0-alpha.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli && sudo mv kagenti-cli /usr/local/bin/

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to the local bin directory.
commands: curl -Lo kagenti-cli https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.4.0-alpha.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli && sudo mv kagenti-cli /usr/local/bin/

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using the Kagenti CLI with specified version and node count.

```bash

kagenti create cluster --name kagenti-cluster --version 1.30 --node-count 3 --region us-central1

```

> Replace 'us-central1' with your preferred region.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

kagenti kubeconfig --name kagenti-cluster

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig to use the new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for your Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.4.0-alpha.1/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.4.0-alpha.1/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command applies the autoscaling configuration.
> This command applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti deployment and warns about potential data loss.

```bash

kagenti delete cluster --name kagenti-cluster

rm -f /usr/local/bin/kagenti-cli

```

> This command deletes the cluster and all associated resources.
> This command removes the Kagenti CLI binary from your system.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Backup your current configuration, upgrade the Kagenti CLI, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get all --namespace kagenti > kagenti-backup.yaml

curl -Lo kagenti-cli https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.4.0-alpha.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli && sudo mv kagenti-cli /usr/local/bin/

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup current resources.
> Upgrade the Kagenti CLI.
> Verify that all nodes are still in 'Ready' status after the upgrade.
commands: curl -Lo kagenti-cli https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.4.0-alpha.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli && sudo mv kagenti-cli /usr/local/bin/

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient permissions or quota in the specified region.

**Fix:**
Check your IAM permissions and resource quotas in the cloud provider.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubectl command-line tool is not installed.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl using 'curl -LO https://storage.googleapis.com/kubernetes-release/release/$(curl -s https://storage.googleapis.com/kubernetes-release/release/stable.txt)/bin/linux/amd64/kubectl && chmod +x kubectl && sudo mv kubectl /usr/local/bin/'

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubeconfig context not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig file is not set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'kagenti kubeconfig --name kagenti-cluster' to set up the kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Node not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Nodes may not have sufficient resources or are not properly initialized.

**Fix:**
Check node logs with 'kubectl describe node <node-name>' and ensure nodes meet resource requirements.

versionNotes.changes: Initial release with support for AI agent orchestration, zero-trust security architecture, and standardized agent communication protocols.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No migration steps required for this initial release.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' status.
codeSnippets: curl -Lo kagenti-cli https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.4.0-alpha.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli && sudo mv kagenti-cli /usr/local/bin/

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
